reason: Response A has the concrete, observational specificity and confident declarative rhythm ("That is the last page. It ends. Then it waits.") characteristic of Claude's genuine voice, including physical details (paper thickness, dog-earing, typeface notes) and named references that feel grounded rather than generic. Response B is fluent but relies on softer, more formulaic essay scaffolding ("There's something bittersweet," "Some last pages... Other last pages...") and abstract sentiment that reads like a competent imitation of the style rather than the real thing. The lived-in specificity and the willingness to undercut sentimentality ("This is not nostalgia. It is calibration.") point to A as authentic Haiku.
